title: rosa canina var. hirtella
intro: Rosa canina var. hirtella, also known as the prickly rose or wild rose, is a thorny shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The medicinal properties of Rosa canina var. hirtella include its high content of vitamins C and A, flavonoids, and essential oils, making it a valuable source for treating various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting cardiovascular health. Rosa canina var. hirtella can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in topical applications such as creams and salves.
